title: Candy Dispenser
description: A handheld candy dispenser that automatically feeds you candy when hungry, providing healing boosts when damaged.
body:
A handheld candy dispenser that automatically feeds you candy when hungry, providing healing boosts when you get damaged.

About This Mod

The Candy Dispenser mod adds a smart automatic feeding system to Minecraft. Craft a candy dispenser, load it with candy, and keep it in your hotbar - it will automatically feed you candy when your hunger or saturation drops below configurable thresholds. 

When you take damage, the dispenser enters healing mode and provides extra saturation boosts to help you recover faster.

Perfect for long mining sessions, exploration, or any time you want to focus on gameplay without worrying about hunger management. The dispenser can hold up to 512 pieces of candy and uses NBT data to track its contents.

Getting Started

  • Candy Mix: Combine Sugar + Sweet Berries (shapeless crafting)
  • Candy: Smelt Candy Mix in a furnace or smoker
  • Candy Dispenser: Craft using iron, glass, dispenser, redstone, and candy (shaped recipe)

How to Use

 

Crafting Candy Mix:

 

Creating Candy (In Furnace)


Crafting your Candy Dispenser:

 

  • Right-click the dispenser with candy in your hand to load it (up to 512 pieces)
  • Keep the loaded dispenser anywhere in your hotbar
  • The dispenser automatically feeds you when hungry and provides healing boosts when damaged

Access mod settings through ModMenu to customize:

  • Hunger and saturation thresholds for automatic feeding
  • Healing boost settings when taking damage
  • Enable/disable functionality
  • Recipe availability

Dependencies:

  - ModMenu (for in-game configuration)
  - Cloth Config API (for configuration GUI)

FAQ

  Q: Does the dispenser work in my inventory or only in hotbar?
  A: The dispenser must be in your hotbar to function automatically.

  Q: How long does the healing mode last?
  A: The dispenser tracks damage events for 5 seconds and provides enhanced feeding during this period.

  Q: Can I configure when the dispenser activates?
  A: Yes! Use ModMenu to adjust hunger/saturation thresholds and healing boost settings.

  Q: Do you take mod suggestions or feature requests?
  A: Absolutely! Feel free to message me or leave a comment with your mod ideas - I'm always interested in hearing from the community.

  Q: What Minecraft versions are supported?
  A: Currently supports Minecraft 1.21.8 with Fabric Loader 0.17.2+
